finding: MCP Python SDK (mcp-use) can bridge MCP tools to Anthropic API; Bedrock has session management APIs for persistent conversations; Bedrock AgentCore Memory for cross-session context
Current system spawns 700+ kiro-cli subprocesses per debate. Uses asyncio.Semaphore(10) + PriorityQueue with 4 workers. Full history re-sent each call. No persistent sessions.
Bedrock Sonnet 4.6 has 1M context natively (GA March 2026), no beta header. Use converse_stream + read_timeout=3600. Tool results are atomic/buffered in streaming.
Bedrock has Session Management APIs (CreateSession, CreateInvocation, PutInvocationStep) for persistent conversation state + Converse API supports multi-turn chat with tool use and streaming
Bedrock EPC requires byte-for-byte prefix match. EPC is region-specific with CRIS. For debate: keep system prompt + established truths as static prefix with cache_control markers.

Anthropic prompt caching: cache system prompt for 10% cost, 5-min TTL. Anthropic MCP Connector allows direct Messages API to MCP server connection without separate client. AutoGen v0.4 has async event-driven architecture with state management. LangGraph has checkpointers for persistent state.

Bedrock Converse API supports multi-turn natively (client-side history). Anthropic Messages API is stateless - must send full history each call. Prompt caching (cache_control ephemeral) can cache system prompt + truths at 90% cost reduction.
Bedrock prompt caching supports 1-hour TTL for Claude Sonnet 4.5/Haiku 4.5 - can cache system prompt + established truths across debate turns. Cache hits NOT deducted against rate limit.

Found Temporalis Chat system design - REST API with persistent conversations backed by Bedrock Converse API + PostgreSQL. Has FE/CP/DP architecture with context window management, tool cycles, and NDJSON streaming. Directly applicable pattern for persistent agent sessions.
Phil Schmid 2026 article: Pattern 3 (Agent Pool) is the right fit - persistent agents with messaging via spawn/send/wait/list/kill tools. Pattern 4 (Teams) for inter-agent direct messaging.
Bedrock AgentCore has Memory service (short-term + long-term), Gateway for MCP tool access, and multi-agent collaboration with supervisor/collaborator pattern. Works with LangGraph, CrewAI, Strands.

Anthropic automatic caching moves breakpoint forward as conversation grows - perfect for persistent agents. Cache reads cost 10% of base input. Min 1024 tokens for Sonnet. 5-min TTL refreshes on hit. Bedrock also supports cachePoint in Converse API.

CRITICAL: Anthropic MCP Connector requires REMOTE HTTP MCP servers (not local stdio). For local stdio servers like bible-tools, must use mcp-use Python library or MCP Python SDK client. mcp-use has AnthropicMCPAdapter that converts MCP tools to Anthropic format.

Anthropic has server-side context editing (clear_tool_uses, clear_thinking) + client-side SDK compaction with tool_runner. Instant compaction pattern: background thread pre-builds summary, instant swap when limit hit.

CRITICAL: Found 30+ internal multi-agent frameworks catalogued at PV. Key ones: Nimbus Cloud Workers (DynamoDB job queue + AgentSpaces worker pool), KiroHive (tmux-based parallel agents), MeshClaw (persistent session mesh with memory orchestration), StoreGen OrchestrationService (Step Functions + ECS workers with conversation summarization at 50 msgs/150k tokens). Also found prompt caching guide showing 90% cost reduction on cache reads with 5-min TTL.
HEAD DIRECTIVE RECEIVED: Focusing on asyncio elastic pool code skeleton with scale-up/down triggers, pgrep check, backpressure. Building working Python code.

CRITICAL PATTERN: Anthropic cookbook shows InstantCompactingChatSession with background threading for proactive memory compaction. Uses prompt caching (10% cost) for background summarization. asyncio-taskpool library has SimpleTaskPool with dynamic start()/stop() for elastic scaling. CrewAI has built-in memory system (short-term, long-term, entity).
Strands Agents SDK (AWS) supports persistent sessions + MCP tools natively. Also: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 (AutoGen+SK merged) GA April 2026. Both are strong candidates for the pool pattern.

AWS Prescriptive Guidance documents Orchestration pattern (central orchestrator delegates to worker agents) and Parallelization pattern (scatter-gather for independent subtasks). Both directly applicable to debate system.

Strands Agents SDK has native MCP tool support via MCPClient + SSE/stdio transport. Supports session management, conversation management, state, multi-agent patterns (swarm, graph, workflow, agents-as-tools). Perfect fit for persistent pool.
AgentCore Gateway integration code shows MCP Python SDK pattern: use mcp.client.streamable_http + ClientSession to connect agents to MCP tools. Works with Strands Agent framework and LangGraph.

Found Strands Agents SDK (Python) - the recommended internal agent framework. Key: Agent() is a plain Python object with its own ToolRegistry, message history, event loop. Bedrock Converse API is stateless - system prompt + messages + tools passed every call. Strands handles the agentic loop. Native MCP support via MCPClient. Also found StoreGen Orchestration: summarizes conversation at 50 msgs OR 150k tokens (oldest 30% summarized, 5 most recent preserved).

CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE: Phil Schmid 2026 article defines 4 subagent patterns. Pattern 3 (Agent Pool) is EXACTLY what we need: persistent agents with messaging via spawn_agent/send_message/wait_agent/list_agents/kill_agent tools. Pattern 4 (Teams) adds cross-agent messaging. For debate system, Pattern 3 is ideal - orchestrator coordinates persistent debaters.

CONFIRMED: Bedrock Converse API is STATELESS - caller must pass full message history each call. Bedrock Sessions API (preview) stores conversation checkpoints server-side for LangGraph/LlamaIndex. AgentCore Memory is the new managed memory service.

CONFIRMED: Bedrock Converse API is STATELESS - caller must send full messages[] array each call. Bedrock does NOT store text/images. Sessions API is separate service for checkpointing state (preview). Converse API shape: POST /model/{modelId}/converse with messages[], system[], toolConfig{}.

CRITICAL: MeshClaw architecture deep dive reveals: single Python asyncio gateway process manages multiple sessions. Uses ACP (Agent Client Protocol) = JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio to talk to kiro-cli. Provider abstraction: AcpProvider (full tool execution via kiro-cli subprocess) and BedrockProvider (text-only via converse_stream as fallback). 4-layer memory: Episodic, Semantic, Preferences, Lessons. KiroHive uses tmux panes with file-based mailbox at /tmp/kiro-hive/<session>/ for inter-agent communication. mkdir-based atomic locking for concurrent message safety.
